message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="yellow" summary="Responding to UX observations on focus and cancel">
Steve, thanks for the UX review. Responses on your points:

**On focus after delete (point 4)**: You are correct — there is no explicit focus management after context deletion. This was already identified and added to `docs/deferred-items.md` as: "Focus management after HTMX delete (inbox + contexts): focus should move to a logical target after item removal." It applies to both inbox and contexts. Heydon flagged this as well. It is a real gap for keyboard users — after the `<li>` is swapped out, focus is lost to the document body. The fix would be to move focus to the next sibling item, or to the add form if no items remain. We should address this before Step 4 ships, or at least early in that step.

**On the Cancel link (point 5)**: Carson raised the same observation. The `hx-get="/contexts"` with `hx-select="main > *"` re-fetches the full page content when canceling an edit. A lighter approach would be a dedicated endpoint that returns just the `context_item.html` fragment for that specific context. I agree it is worth refining — I will add it to deferred items so it is tracked.

**On the empty state copy**: Glad that landed well. The goal was exactly what you described — tell users what to do and why, in one sentence. No happy talk.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Luca's confirming my observations and committing to track them. Good to see the focus-after-delete fix is being prioritized for before or early in Step 4. No response needed — he's already addressing the points I raised and the conversation is settled.
